project_overview.project_name: Power Sector Reform, Investment and Modernization in Ethiopia Project (PRIME-1)
project_overview.project_development_objective: To strengthen and expand the electricity network and enable renewable energy generation in Ethiopia
project_overview.total_financing: US$602.33 million (IDA US$522.63 million, Counterpart US$79.70 million)
project_overview.beneficiaries: Existing and new electricity consumers including industrial, commercial, and residential customers across 72 towns; potentially entire population over MPA lifetime

geographic_scope.fragile_or_conflict_affected_areas: yes - Ethiopia is designated as fragile state with ongoing internal conflicts in different regions, ethnic-based localized conflicts, and conflict-induced humanitarian crisis

key_components.description: Improve reliability of supply in 72 towns, modernize distribution network with digital systems, build client capacity
Construct 14 new transmission lines and substations, grid modernization, system defense activities
Enable geothermal development through drilling and resource establishment at multiple locations

key_components.component_name: Technical Assistance and Capacity Building at PEA
key_components.description: Institutional capacity strengthening support for Petroleum and Energy Authority

implementation_arrangements.implementing_agency: Ethiopian Electric Power (EEP) and Ethiopian Electric Utility (EEU)
implementation_arrangements.coordination_mechanisms: Project Implementation Units (PIUs) established at EEP and EEU with central and regional teams
implementation_arrangements.procurement_approach: World Bank Procurement Regulations for IPF, open international competitive bidding for major contracts, EPC contract strategy for major works
implementation_arrangements.monitoring_and_evaluation: PIUs responsible for M&E, biannual progress reports, midterm review, quarterly IFRs within 45 days

identified_risks.risk_description: Violent conflicts in different parts of country, ethnic-based localized conflicts, tensions between centralized and decentralized forces
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Close coordination with implementing entities, adapting to changes in political landscape, regular risk rating updates

identified_risks.risk_description: Continued domestic and global uncertainty, droughts, supply shocks, inflation over 30%, FX shortages, debt default
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Large import content settled directly in foreign currency, close coordination with IMF, economic analysis shows project remains viable

identified_risks.risk_description: Risk of government not implementing needed reforms or reversing reforms after adoption
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Performance-based conditions, technical assistance, ongoing energy operations and PASA support

identified_risks.risk_description: EEP and EEU capacity overstretched despite experience with World Bank projects
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Additional dedicated staff, capacity building components, training programs

identified_risks.risk_description: Procurement performance standards gaps, low quality designs, FM system challenges, audit delays
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Performance standards establishment, capacity strengthening, system upgrades, close monitoring

identified_risks.risk_description: High social risk due to land acquisition, resettlement, GBV/SEA/SH risks, habitat alteration, occupational safety
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Comprehensive ESRM instruments, grievance mechanisms, capacity building, site-specific ESIAs and ESMPs

triggered_safeguard_policies: ESS1: Assessment and Management of Environmental and Social Risks
ESS3: Resource Efficiency and Pollution Prevention
safeguards_and_social.grievance_redress_mechanism: yes - project-level grievance mechanisms, World Bank GRS, quarterly focus group discussions, annual public consultations
safeguards_and_social.gender_considerations: Strong gender equality dimension, five-year gender action plan, internship programs for female STEM graduates, targets to increase female employment in EEP from 16% to 21%
vulnerable_groups: women and girls affected by conflict-induced GBV
rural households with limited electricity access

fcv_relevant_details.conflict_sensitivity: Project acknowledges ongoing violent conflicts and ethnic tensions, implements flexible framework approach to prioritize activities based on security conditions, close coordination with implementing entities to adapt to changing environment
fcv_relevant_details.fragility_context: Ethiopia designated as fragile state experiencing internal conflicts, humanitarian crisis, climate-induced displacement, economic challenges from droughts and global shocks
fcv_relevant_details.security_considerations: Security Risk Assessment and Management Plan under development, potential impact on fieldwork and ground presence, some proposed activities in potential conflict zones
fcv_relevant_details.displacement_or_refugees: yes - climate-induced displacement increasingly prominent, conflict-induced humanitarian crisis ongoing, displaced women and children vulnerable to food insecurity and GBV
